edges.description: Circulating leptospiral endotoxin engages host Na/K-ATPase during systemic infection.
Cytosolic DNA sensing in macrophages activates a host-defense interferon program during systemic infection.
Dysregulation of acute immune activation contributes to multi-organ tissue injury and the spectrum of severe manifestations.

edges.description: TLR2-linked signaling drives increased cytokine and chemokine programs.
edges.source: TLR2 signaling activation in monocytes and macrophages

edges.description: Cytokine-rich innate activation is associated with inflammasome pathway engagement.
Inflammasome activation promotes IL-1beta production as a downstream inflammatory step.
Increased IL-1beta and systemic inflammatory signaling contribute to fever.
Systemic inflammatory mediator elevation contributes to common constitutional headache.
Factor H-assisted complement control via TolC reduces terminal complement activity.
Reduced complement-mediated killing supports persistence in host tissues.
Persistent colonization enables continuing systemic dissemination.
Direct GLP-Na/K-ATPase interaction impairs pump function across vulnerable host cell types.

edges.description: Cell-level Na/K-ATPase dysfunction propagates to tissue-level organ injury.

edges.description: Coagulation derangement manifests clinically as disseminated intravascular coagulation.

nodes.description: Renal injury is a classic and clinically important complication, especially in severe disease.

evidence.snippet: Leptospirosis is responsible for various clinical syndromes, classically linked with fever and acute kidney injury.

The spectrum of symptoms is variable and usually ranges from asymptomatic cases to severe manifestations involving multiple organ dysfunction accompanied by jaundice, hemorrhage, meningitis, and acute kidney injury that requires the need for intensive care assistance.

nodes.description: Disseminated leptospiral infection triggers acute systemic immune activation characterized by broad upregulation of inflammatory mediators including TNF signaling, type I interferon responses, neutrophil activation, and acute-phase host factors. This response encompasses both protective host-defense mechanisms and pathogenic inflammatory dysregulation that contributes to severity spectrum and multi-organ phenotypes.
nodes.id: Acute systemic immune activation with TNF and interferon signaling

biological_processes: response to molecule of bacterial origin

evidence.snippet: Transcriptomic profiling identified over 5,800 differentially expressed genes, predominantly upregulated, and associated with inflammatory signaling, including tumor necrosis factor signaling, interferon responses, and cytokine-mediated immune activation.

Comparative analysis identified 16 consistently upregulated host factors enriched in pathways related to neutrophil activation, hemostasis, and cytokine signaling. Quantitative RT-PCR confirmed increased expression of SERPINA1, BASP1, ORM1, NAMPT, and GCA, supporting their roles in inflammatory signaling and acute-phase immune responses.
Accumulating evidence indicates that these severe clinical manifestations are closely associated with dysregulated host immune and inflammatory responses triggered during Leptospira infection.

nodes.description: Antibiotics are central treatment across mild and severe presentations, with early therapy improving outcomes.

evidence.snippet: Emphasis is placed on antibiotic therapy, including recommendations for mild and severe cases, as well as the role of probiotics in modulating the gut microbiota.

Although early antibiotic treatment is usually effective, in severe cases, it may require renal replacement therapy, invasive mechanical ventilation, vasoactive support, and invasive hemodynamic monitoring, increasing the risk of death.

nodes.description: Pathogenic leptospires traverse endothelial and epithelial cell barriers during invasive infection.
nodes.id: Barrier transcytosis across endothelial and epithelial monolayers

evidence.reference_title: Identification of collagenase as a critical virulence factor for invasiveness and transmission of pathogenic Leptospira species.
evidence.snippet: Compared with wild-type strain, ΔcolA mutant displayed much-attenuated transcytosis through HEK293 and HUVEC monolayers

nodes.description: Severe leptospirosis can involve dysregulated coagulation with disseminated intravascular coagulation.

evidence.snippet: Additional complications included diffuse intravascular coagulation, necrotizing pancreatitis, and limb ischemia.

nodes.description: Pathogenic Leptospira expresses collagenase activity that degrades host collagen substrates during invasive infection.

evidence.reference_title: Identification of collagenase as a critical virulence factor for invasiveness and transmission of pathogenic Leptospira species.
evidence.snippet: Recombinant or native ColA hydrolyzed all the tested substrates in which type III collagen was the favorite substrate with 2.16 mg/mL Km and 35.6 h(-)(1) Kcat values.

nodes.description: Pro-inflammatory cytokine and chemokine expression increases during acute leptospirosis.

evidence.snippet: Notably, we observed the secretion/mRNA expression of several cytokines (IL6, IL8, IL-1β, TNFα, IFNγ, IL10, CCL2/MCP-1, CCL10, COX2, CXCL1/KC, CXCL2/MIP2) and immune effectors (hBD2, iNOS, Fibronectin, Oxygen, and Nitrogen reactive species) as key aspects of host TLR2 responses during leptospirosis.

nodes.description: Diffuse intravascular coagulation is reported among complications in critical severe leptospirosis.

evidence.snippet: Additional complications included diffuse intravascular coagulation, necrotizing pancreatitis, and limb ischemia.

nodes.description: Fever is a common presenting feature of acute leptospirosis.

evidence.snippet: Leptospirosis is responsible for various clinical syndromes, classically linked with fever and acute kidney injury.

nodes.description: Headache is among the most frequent symptoms in PCR-confirmed acute leptospirosis.

evidence.snippet: The most frequent symptoms in patients with a positive PCR test were headache (91.9%; 34/37), chills and sweating (80.6%; 29/37), nausea (75%; 27/37), dizziness (74.3%; 26/37), vomiting (61.1%; 22/37), congestion (56.8%; 21/37), and conjunctival suffusion (51.4%; 19/37).

nodes.description: Pathogenic Leptospira disseminate through blood during acute systemic infection.

biological_processes: response to molecule of bacterial origin

evidence.snippet: The spectrum of symptoms is variable and usually ranges from asymptomatic cases to severe manifestations involving multiple organ dysfunction accompanied by jaundice, hemorrhage, meningitis, and acute kidney injury that requires the need for intensive care assistance.

nodes.description: Interleukin-1 beta production increases during innate immune activation in leptospirosis.

evidence.snippet: Notably, we observed the secretion/mRNA expression of several cytokines (IL6, IL8, IL-1β, TNFα, IFNγ, IL10, CCL2/MCP-1, CCL10, COX2, CXCL1/KC, CXCL2/MIP2) and immune effectors (hBD2, iNOS, Fibronectin, Oxygen, and Nitrogen reactive species) as key aspects of host TLR2 responses during leptospirosis.

nodes.description: Jaundice is more characteristic of severe disease but not frequent in all confirmed febrile presentations.

evidence.snippet: The frequency of clinical signs classically described in leptospirosis was low: jaundice (8.3%; 3/36) and anuria/oliguria (21.6%; 8/37).

The spectrum of symptoms is variable and usually ranges from asymptomatic cases to severe manifestations involving multiple organ dysfunction accompanied by jaundice, hemorrhage, meningitis, and acute kidney injury that requires the need for intensive care assistance.

nodes.description: Leptospiral glycolipoprotein directly targets host Na/K-ATPase.

evidence.snippet: Na/K-ATPase is the molecular target of the leptospiral endotoxin (glycolipoprotein—GLP).

nodes.description: Leptospira, a spirochete, maintains its peptidoglycan cell wall by penicillin-binding protein (PBP) transpeptidase cross-linking. This is the target of the intravenous penicillin or ceftriaxone used for severe leptospirosis; acylation of the PBP active site halts cross-linking and is bactericidal.

evidence.snippet: although glycan chain polymerization occurs in the absence of transpeptidation (for example, in the presence of penicillin), efficient transpeptidation requires ongoing GTase reactions

nodes.description: Leptospira spp. depend on 70S-ribosome translation. Doxycycline, the first-line agent for mild leptospirosis, binds the 30S subunit and arrests bacterial protein synthesis.

evidence.snippet: The ribosome is one of the main antibiotic targets in the bacterial cell.

nodes.description: Severe leptospirosis causes clinically significant injury across kidney, liver, and lung.
nodes.id: Multi-organ tissue injury in severe leptospirosis

evidence.snippet: The spectrum of symptoms is variable and usually ranges from asymptomatic cases to severe manifestations involving multiple organ dysfunction accompanied by jaundice, hemorrhage, meningitis, and acute kidney injury that requires the need for intensive care assistance.

evidence.reference_title: Alleviation of renal and pulmonary injury by immunomodulation in leptospirosis: hamster model.
evidence.snippet: The autopsy findings revealed severe interstitial nephritis and severe pulmonary hemorrhage.

nodes.description: Leptospirosis-associated molecular stimuli activate NLRP3 inflammasome assembly.

evidence.snippet: Synergistically, multiple molecular stimuli are prompted during the infection, activating inflammasomes and Na/K-ATPase signalosome, leading to pro-inflammatory and metabolic alterations during leptospirosis.

nodes.description: Na/K-ATPase dysfunction in vascular and renal cells drives inflammatory-metabolic dysregulation.
nodes.id: Na/K-ATPase dysfunction in endothelial and kidney epithelial cells

evidence.snippet: Na/K-ATPase dysfunctions on different types of cells give rise to the organ disorders manifested in leptospirosis.

Synergistically, multiple molecular stimuli are prompted during the infection, activating inflammasomes and Na/K-ATPase signalosome, leading to pro-inflammatory and metabolic alterations during leptospirosis.

nodes.description: In vitro evidence supports a model in which immune evasion promotes ongoing leptospiral tissue colonization and virulence.

evidence.snippet: These findings indicate that TolC contributes to leptospiral virulence by promoting host tissue colonization and evading the immune response, presenting it as a potential target for diagnostic and therapeutic strategies.

nodes.description: Pulmonary hemorrhage is a severe complication linked to delayed diagnosis and high-risk disease.

evidence.reference_title: Leptospirosis: An important zoonosis acquired through work, play and travel.
evidence.snippet: Delays in treatment could increase the risk of severe complications, including pulmonary haemorrhage, acute renal failure and acute liver failure.
The presence of complications was 21.6% (8/37), with pulmonary complications being the most frequent (75.0% 6/8).

nodes.description: Hemorrhagic leptospirosis is associated with prominent injury to the lung microcirculation.
nodes.id: Pulmonary microvascular damage in hemorrhagic leptospirosis

evidence.reference_title: Human hemorrhagic pulmonary leptospirosis: pathological findings and pathophysiological correlations.
evidence.snippet: Prominent vascular damage was present in the lung microcirculation, with decreased CD34 and preserved aquaporin 1 expression.

nodes.description: Complement terminal pathway activity is reduced by TolC-dependent immune evasion.

evidence.snippet: The rTolC protein inhibited both the alternative and the classical pathway-mediated membrane attack complex (MAC) deposition in vitro.

nodes.description: TLR2-dependent innate signaling is activated in myeloid cells during leptospirosis.
nodes.id: TLR2 signaling activation in monocytes and macrophages

evidence.snippet: We assessed the direct TLR2 expression and indirect TLR2 involvement via the secretion/mRNA expression of immune effectors during leptospirosis.

Even though increased TLR2 expression in in-vivo and in-vitro studies was evident, human studies reported mixed results showing that the postulated effect of TLR2 response based on other studies may not be valid for human leptospirosis.

nodes.description: Leptospiral TolC interacts with factor H and preserves factor H cofactor activity.

evidence.snippet: Functional assays demonstrated that rTolC-bound FH retained cofactor activity for C3b cleavage, highlighting TolC’s role in complement regulation.

nodes.description: Virulent leptospires disrupt endothelial VE-cadherin localization in adherens junctions, linking host-cell association to vascular barrier injury.
nodes.id: VE-cadherin adherens junction disruption in endothelial cells

evidence.reference_title: In vitro and in vivo endothelial interactions of Leptospira species are markers of virulence.
evidence.snippet: Additionally, bacterial association with host cells correlates with the loss of VE-cadherin localization in adherens junctions.

evidence.reference_title: In vitro and in vivo endothelial interactions of Leptospira species are markers of virulence.
evidence.snippet: Our findings indicate that VE-cadherin disruption correlates with P1 + species and the presence of virulence-associated genes.

nodes.description: Secreted virulence-modifying proteins exploit host calcium signaling to destabilize epithelial junctions and enable early barrier breach.

evidence.reference_title: In vivo dual RNA-Seq uncovers key effectors of epithelial barrier disruption by an extracellular pathogen.
evidence.snippet: We demonstrate that, upon infection, an increase in intracellular calcium triggers tight junction destabilization, by activating the calmodulin and myosin light chain kinase signalization.

evidence.reference_title: In vivo dual RNA-Seq uncovers key effectors of epithelial barrier disruption by an extracellular pathogen.
evidence.snippet: We identify two bacterial effectors of the Virulence-Modifying (VM) proteins family, structurally related to toxin-like proteins, that promote modulation of calcium homeostasis and disruption of cell-cell junctions, thereby allowing Leptospira translocation across epithelium barriers, tissue colonization and pathogenicity.

nodes.description: Cytosolic DNA sensing in macrophages triggers cGAS-STING signaling that helps restrain renal colonization during leptospiral infection.

evidence.reference_title: cGAS-STING dependent type I IFN reduces Leptospira interrogans renal colonization in mice.
evidence.snippet: We show that L. interrogans induces a robust type I interferon (IFN) response in human and murine macrophages that is dependent on the cytosolic dsDNA sensor Cyclic GMP-AMP Synthase (cGAS) and the Stimulator of IFN Genes (STING) signaling pathway.

evidence.reference_title: cGAS-STING dependent type I IFN reduces Leptospira interrogans renal colonization in mice.
evidence.snippet: Further, we show that mice deficient in the IFNα/β receptor subunit 1 (IFNAR1) or STING had higher bacterial burdens and increased renal colonization following infection in vivo suggesting that cGAS-STING-driven type I IFN is required for the host defense against L. interrogans.
